data.title: Booz Allen targets $900M–$1B free cash flow for FY26 as tech transformation accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Booz Allen Hamilton Holding Corporation (BAH) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Horacio D. Rozanski stated that "our first quarter performance played out as we expected," highlighting book-to-bill and record backlog as the brightest spots. Rozanski emphasized the company is accelerating its transformation in response to a dynamic government environment, noting, "We expect the full effects of the civil sector reset to manifest in our Q2 financials and intend to return to growth in the back half of this year."
  • Rozanski detailed restructuring and a reset in the Civil business, including rightsizing talent and a focus on modernization, citing a $51 million task order with Customs and Border Protection using AWS cloud technology. The CEO also outlined consolidation of the Defense Technology Group and highlighted a $315 million Air Force contract for the Tactical Operations Center Light Battle Management System, stating, "We are going to deploy TOC-L to 70 locations around the world, including Europe and the Pacific."
  • Rozanski described the company’s VoLT strategy—velocity, leadership, and technology—alongside deepening partnerships across the tech ecosystem and using AI and automation for internal efficiencies.
  • CFO Matthew A. Calderone reported, "The first quarter played out very much as we expected. We delivered growth in revenue ex billables, where most of our profitability is generated, and at the bottom line." Calderone also noted, "We achieved an excellent quarterly book-to-bill of 1.42x and total backlog hit an all-time Q1 record of $38 billion."

Outlook

  • Calderone announced, "We are only updating our full year guidance to reflect the anticipated federal tax impact on our cash flow from the passage of The One Big Beautiful Bill. We now expect free cash flow to be between $900 million and $1 billion."
  • The company anticipates lower revenue and profit growth in the first half of the fiscal year, with expectations to return to growth in the back half as the funding environment normalizes.

Financial Results

  • Calderone reported, "For the first quarter, gross revenue was down roughly 1% year-over-year to $2.9 billion. Revenue, excluding billable expenses, where most of our profitability is generated, grew 2% year-over-year."
  • Defense revenue rose 7% and Intel revenue increased 6% year-over-year, while Civil revenue declined 13%.
  • The company booked $4.2 billion in awards, resulting in a 1.42x book-to-bill ratio. Total backlog reached $38 billion, up 11% year-over-year, and the proposal pipeline was nearly $43 billion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $311 million, a 3% increase, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 10.6%. Net income was $271 million, up 64% due to a $106 million one-time income tax benefit. Adjusted net income was $184 million, up 2%.
  • Diluted earnings per share was $2.16, and adjusted diluted earnings per share was $1.48. Free cash flow for the quarter was $96 million.
  • Booz Allen repurchased just over 1% of shares and increased its commitment to Booz Allen Ventures by $200 million.

Q&A

  • Michael Louie D DiPalma, William Blair: Asked if the procurement environment is better and about tech partnerships. Rozanski responded that "the business has stabilized in what is still a very dynamic environment... the procurement environment has improved, but it's still operating below historical speeds."
  • DiPalma also inquired about fixed-price contracts. Calderone replied, "We've been talking about our desire to move to more outcome-based and fixed-price contracts for years now. So it certainly has a potential to be a win-win for everybody."
  • Gautam J. Khanna, TD Cowen: Asked about funded backlog, headcount targets, and Advana contract impacts. Calderone explained the decline in funded backlog is due to slow funding but not a concern, and Kristine Martin Anderson said hiring is paced to demand and that they are confident in meeting headcount guidance.
  • Mariana Perez Mora, BofA Securities: Asked about visibility and exposure to Golden Dome and Civil revenue headwinds. Rozanski indicated Booz Allen is well-positioned for Golden Dome and expects to play multiple roles. Anderson noted Civil is stable after the reset, with confidence in future growth.
  • Colin Michael Canfield, Cantor: Queried nonoperational cash flow and venture investment tailwinds. Calderone explained the $200 million and $170 million cash tax benefits and said venture returns are promising but not yet realized.
  • Sheila Karin Kahyaoglu, Jefferies: Asked about long-term headcount and productivity. Rozanski and Anderson emphasized technology and AI will increase productivity, with the expectation that revenue per employee should rise over time.
  • Jonathan Siegmann, Stifel: Asked about regulatory changes and outcome-based contracting. Rozanski highlighted the benefits of FAR rewrites and executive orders accelerating AI and outcome-based contracts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was slightly positive, with questions probing headcount stability, procurement pace, and opportunities in new contracts, while often expressing optimism about the company’s tech positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing resilience and strategic clarity. Rozanski stated, "We feel very good about that. And our teams are working very closely with each of our customers."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a constructive stance, though management’s confidence in transformation and funding normalization increased.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for free cash flow was raised to $900 million–$1 billion from $700 million–$800 million previously, reflecting positive tax law changes.
  • Defense and Intel businesses showed continued revenue growth, while Civil’s decline moderated after a significant reset last quarter.
  • Book-to-bill improved to 1.42x from the prior quarter’s 0.71x, and backlog reached a new Q1 record.
  • Management tone was more assertive on transformation initiatives and technology leadership, while analysts remained focused on funding and headcount stabilization.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward faster adoption of AI, outcome-based contracts, and leveraging Booz Allen Ventures.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing uncertainty in the funding environment, with procurement speeds still below historical levels.
  • The timing of a return to normalized funding and hiring remains a risk.
  • Analysts asked about headwinds in Civil revenue and the potential pace of contract funding releases.
  • Management is mitigating risks through rightsizing, technology-driven productivity gains, and close alignment with customer missions.

Final Takeaway

Booz Allen Hamilton’s first quarter of fiscal 2026 reflected expected dynamics, with a Civil business reset largely completed and strong momentum in Defense and Intel. Management’s focus is on accelerating transformation, leveraging technology partnerships, and maximizing shareholder value as the company projects free cash flow between $900 million and $1 billion for the year. As the funding environment normalizes, Booz Allen aims to return to growth in the second half, supported by record backlog and a robust pipeline of mission-critical technology opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tilray Brands Q4 Earnings Preview: What to expect
data.description:
When Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) reports fourth quarter earnings on Monday, Wall Street expects the firm to post EPS of -$0.03, while revenue is expected to increase 1.5% to $233.29 million during the quarter.

The Canadian cannabis company fell short of Street forecasts for its revenue in the past three consecutive quarters. It also revised its full-year outlook to indicate $850M-$900M in revenue for fiscal 2025, down from previous guidance of $950M-$1B.

Seeking Alpha analyst Alan Brochstein downgraded TLRY to Neutral from Strong Buy, arguing that revenue outlook has weakened, with FY26 and FY27 estimates declining, despite a modest improvement in adjusted EBITDA projections.

Tilray Brands slipped over 47% so far this year, underperforming the broader S&P Index, which gained over 8% during the same period.

As per the Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, TLRY has a Hold rating with a score of 2.61 out of 5. The company received an A for growth and a B grade for profitability, but a C- grade in momentum as well as revisions dragged down the rating.

Earlier this month, the U.S. Drug Enforcement Agency (DEA) informed an in-house judge this week that a regulatory process to reschedule marijuana initiated under the Biden Administration remains on hold at the agency.

“Tilray Brands is a high-risk, high-reward opportunity after a 99%+ decline over seven years,” noted Seeking Alpha analyst Paul Franke, adding that, “Potential U.S. regulatory changes under President Trump could dramatically boost industry sentiment and Tilray's prospects.”

Over the last two years, TLRY has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 38% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen no upward revisions versus one downward move, while revenue estimates have seen no upward revisions against four downward revisions.

data.title: Raymond James upgrades MSCI on 'traits that merit a premium valuation'
data.description:
Market indexes and investment data provider MSCI (NYSE:MSCI) was upgraded at Raymond James on "traits that merit a premium valuation".

"Importantly, we believe MSCI's index subscription pricing power is very much intact, owing to the sticky nature of active asset manager benchmarks, and revenue retention in MSCI's Index segment has normalized after a hiccup in 2024," said analysts Patrick O'Shaughnessy and David Farnum.

"In addition, the long-standing shift towards passive investment vehicles continues to benefit MSCI. As a result, we believe the stock’s current valuation creates an attractive entry point and risk/reward," added the analysts.

Raymond James raised its recommendation on the stock to Outperform from Market Perform, and set the price target at $650.00.

The stock was +0.60% during Friday morning trade to $546.56.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors ratings of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Hold.

data.title: AstraZeneca gets EU backing for eco-friendly inhaler version
data.description:
• AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) has received a positive endorsement from the European Medicines Agency (EMA) for a new eco-friendly version of its inhaler, Trixeo Aerosphere. 
  • The EMA's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use has recommended the use of a new type of pressurized gas in this triple-drug inhaler.
  • This recommendation is based on clinical studies showing that the new propellant is equivalent in effectiveness to the current one used in Trixeo.
  • Trixeo Aerosphere is already approved for treating chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in adults in the EU.

data.title: Wall Street treads water after S&P 500 and Nasdaq tagged fresh closing highs
data.description:
Wall Street is muted on Friday, with the major averages looking for clear direction as investors digest the previous session’s record-setting finishes for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) hovers near even as it conitues to eye its own record high. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has moved up by 0.1% and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has gained 0.1% so far on the session. 

From a sector perspective, eight of the S&P 500’s 11 segments are in the green, led by strong performances in Consumer Discretionary and Industrials. On the flip side, Real Estate is lagging behind as the weakest performer of the day. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields are relatively muted with the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rising by 1 basis point to 4.41%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) sits near even at 3.91%. 

Looking out at the economic calendar, durable goods dropped less than feared in June, with new orders for manufactured durable goods: -9.3% M/M to $311.8B vs. -10.4% consensus and +16.5% prior (revised from +16.4%) 

On the earnings end, Intel (INTC) shares are down 9.4% after the company's bottom line in the second quarter missed Wall Street estimates. The firm's Q3 outlook also failed to impress investors. The company also announced a 15% workforce reduction.

data.title: Deutsche Bank cut to Sell at Citi after Q2 earnings fail to impress
data.description:
Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) shares fell 1.7% in Frankfurt trading on Friday after Citi downgraded the bank to Sell from Neutral. Q2 results underscored a wider gap between management commentary and actual earnings, Citi said.

"With the share price up strongly on what we thought were an average set of results and limited evidence of revenue progression in Corporate & Private Bank, we downgrade to Sell/High Risk," analyst Andrew Coombs wrote in a note to clients.

Deutsche Bank (DBK) stock's 7.8% jump after Q2 earnings on Thursday appear to be driven by commentary management provided on regulatory risk-weighted asset inflation risk, the analyst said. That, though, didn't concern Citi. 

Rather, one of Citi's fears is that capital return is now being held to a higher bar, with buybacks now only expected when the CET1 ratio “sustainably exceeds” 14%. Also, the company may be struggling to reach its  ~€32B revenue target for 2025. "The mix of revenues is now projected to be from more cyclical areas compared to the start of this year, so warrants a lower multiple in our opinion," Coombs wrote.

Citi cut its target price on Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB) stock to €24.4 ($28.6) from €25.0 and its 2026-27 revenue forecasts by 1%.

The Sell rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating, both at Hold, and the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy.
